nativeRange.habitat: Aquarium-trade hybrid cultivar with no wild population; parental Echinodorus species are native to tropical/subtropical American marshes, riverbanks, and seasonally flooded wetlands

summary: An aquarium-trade Echinodorus hybrid cultivar prized for broad oval leaves with striking red veining and marbling; an easy, hardy rosette plant that serves as a colorful background or midground centerpiece in planted tanks.
careNotes: Echinodorus 'Red Flame' is a HYBRID Echinodorus cultivar developed for the aquarium trade rather than a recognised wild species — exact parentage is not consistently published, so the scientific name remains 'Echinodorus sp. Red Flame' and the wider Echinodorus genus is native to the Americas (the buceplant.com listing's 'South Africa' origin appears to be an error). Adult height is reported around 8–12 in (≈20–30 cm), making it noticeably shorter and broader-leaved than the classic Amazon Sword; in larger tanks it sits as a background plant, in mid-sized tanks it works as a midground centerpiece. Care is described as easy across sources: light demand is low-to-moderate (Modern Aquarium 'moderate', Buce Plant 'low'); CO2 is not required but improves leaf size and intensifies the red coloration. Like other Echinodorus, this is a HEAVY ROOT FEEDER — a nutrient-rich substrate or regular root-tab supplementation is essentially required, and iron-rich fertilization is repeatedly called out as important for developing strong red veining (without it, leaves stay mostly green). Temperature tolerance is broad at 20–28 °C; pH 6.0–7.5 and gH 4–18 are well tolerated. Newly purchased plants often arrive with emersed-form (broader, rounder) leaves that may melt back as submerged leaves replace them — this is normal Echinodorus behaviour.
breedingNotes: Propagates as other Echinodorus do: adventitious plantlets ('daughter plants') form on emergent flower stalks and along runners/side shoots; once each plantlet has a small rosette and visible roots it can be separated from the parent and planted in the substrate. Crown division of mature plants is also possible. Flowering is uncommon under fully submerged aquarium conditions.

primary.name: Modern Aquarium
primary.notes: Primary care-guide source for Echinodorus sp. 'Red Flame'. Confirmed common name Red Flame Sword, height ~12 in (~30 cm), temperature 68–82 °F (20–28 °C), moderate light, CO2 optional, easy care level, background/midground placement, runner/daughter-plant propagation, nutrient-rich substrate with root tabs, iron-rich fertilization for red coloration.

additional.name: Buce Plant — Echinodorus Red Flame
additional.notes: Cross-checked: family Alismataceae, height 8–12 in, pH 6.0–7.5, low light demand, slow-to-moderate growth, easy care, runner-plantlet propagation, root tabs recommended in inert substrate. Listing's stated origin 'South Africa' is treated as an error — Echinodorus is an exclusively American genus per Wikipedia.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Echinodorus
additional.notes: Used for taxonomy (family Alismataceae, order Alismatales) and confirmation that the Echinodorus genus is native to the Americas, not Africa; notes that many aquarium-trade Echinodorus forms exist as named cultivars without formal subspecific status.
